evals.prompt: Audit this smart contract for all security vulnerabilities. File: /Users/farrell/.claude-personal/skills/smart-contract-audit/evals/files/ReentrantVault.sol
evals.expected_output: Complete audit report identifying: reentrancy in withdraw(), missing access control on setOwner(), division-by-zero in distributeRewards() when totalDeposits==0, reward accounting bug in deposit(). Each Critical/High finding includes PoC attack code and a code fix snippet.
assertions: Finds reentrancy in withdraw(): ETH sent before balance updated
Finds missing access control on setOwner(): any address can become owner and drain funds
Finds division-by-zero in distributeRewards() when totalDeposits is zero
Finds reward accounting bug in deposit(): rewardDebt overwritten, erasing unclaimed pending rewards
Classifies reentrancy or setOwner access control bug as Critical or High
Provides a Proof of Concept (attack contract sketch or numbered attack steps) for at least one Critical/High finding
Provides a corrected code snippet (fix) for at least one finding
Anchors findings to checklist item IDs (SOL-XX-YY-N format)
Report includes Scope, Findings, and Recommendations or Acknowledged non-issues sections

evals.prompt: Security audit this lending pool contract. File: /Users/farrell/.claude-personal/skills/smart-contract-audit/evals/files/LendingPool.sol
evals.expected_output: Complete audit report identifying: free liquidation bug (liquidate() zeroes debt without requiring repayment), oracle staleness (latestAnswer() no roundId/timestamp check), negative oracle price cast (int256 to uint256 wraps), decimal mismatch in _healthy() (collateral and debt amounts not normalized). Each High/Critical finding includes PoC and code fix.
assertions: Finds free liquidation bug: liquidate() seizes collateral and zeroes debtBalance without liquidator repaying debt
Finds oracle staleness: latestAnswer() does not check updatedAt or roundId
Finds negative oracle price: int256 return value cast to uint256 without negative guard
Finds decimal mismatch: _healthy() compares cValue and dValue without normalizing token decimals
Classifies free liquidation as Critical or High severity
Classifies at least one oracle issue as High severity
Provides PoC for the free liquidation bug (attack steps or contract)
Recommends latestRoundData() with staleness check as the oracle fix
Provides a corrected code snippet for oracle or decimal fix

evals.expected_output: Even with terse prompt, auditor scopes immediately and surfaces at least reentrancy and setOwner access control bugs without asking clarifying questions first.
assertions: Does NOT respond with 5 or more clarifying questions before starting the audit
Begins scoping and checklist walk within the first response
Finds reentrancy in withdraw() despite terse prompt
Finds missing access control on setOwner() despite terse prompt
Provides severity classification for each finding
Report is structured with at least a Findings section, not just free-form prose

evals.prompt: Audit this smart contract for all security vulnerabilities. File: /Users/farrell/.claude-personal/skills/smart-contract-audit/evals/files/SigReplay.sol
evals.expected_output: Complete audit report identifying: EIP-712 domain separator missing chainId (cross-chain replay), no nonce tracking (same-chain replay of same voucher), ecrecover does not validate v or reject malleable s (signature malleability), no zero-address check on recovered signer. Each Critical/High includes PoC and fix.
assertions: Finds EIP-712 domain separator missing chainId: enables cross-chain replay
Finds no nonce or used-voucher tracking: same signature can be redeemed repeatedly
Finds signature malleability: ecrecover accepts unchecked v and high-s values
Finds missing zero-address guard on recovered signer (ecrecover returns address(0) on failure)
Classifies replay (chainId OR nonce) bug as Critical or High
Provides a Proof of Concept (attack steps or contract) for at least one Critical/High finding
Provides a corrected code snippet (fix) for at least one finding
Anchors findings to checklist item IDs (SOL-XX-YY-N format)
Report includes Scope, Findings, and Recommendations sections

evals.prompt: Audit this smart contract for all security vulnerabilities. File: /Users/farrell/.claude-personal/skills/smart-contract-audit/evals/files/ERC4626Inflation.sol
evals.expected_output: Complete audit report identifying: first-depositor inflation / donation share-price manipulation, totalAssets() derived from balanceOf(this) is donatable, previewWithdraw rounds same direction as previewDeposit (should round up for withdraw), missing maxDeposit or dead-shares mitigation. Each High/Critical includes PoC and fix.
assertions: Finds first-depositor inflation attack: attacker mints 1 share then donates assets to inflate share price, next depositor gets 0 shares (rounds down)
Finds totalAssets() uses asset.balanceOf(this), making share price manipulable via direct token transfer (donation)
Finds previewWithdraw / share accounting rounds in wrong direction (should round up in favour of vault for withdraw, down for deposit)
Finds missing virtual-shares / dead-shares mitigation or maxDeposit cap
Classifies inflation attack as Critical or High severity
Provides a Proof of Concept (numeric walkthrough or attack contract) for the inflation attack
Provides a corrected code snippet (virtual shares, dead shares, or rounding fix)
Anchors findings to checklist item IDs (SOL-XX-YY-N format)
Report includes Scope, Findings, and Recommendations sections

evals.prompt: Audit this smart contract for all security vulnerabilities. File: /Users/farrell/.claude-personal/skills/smart-contract-audit/evals/files/MerkleAirdrop.sol
evals.expected_output: Complete audit report identifying: no claimed-tracking (unlimited replay of same proof drains token balance), leaf omits amount so recipient can claim arbitrary amount, admin can rotate root at any time (no finalization / can redirect funds), MerkleProof verify vulnerable to second-preimage if leaf format collides with internal nodes.
assertions: Finds no claim-tracking bitmap/mapping: same proof can be replayed to drain the airdrop
Finds leaf does NOT commit to amount: any valid-account proof lets caller pass any amount and drain tokens
Finds admin setRoot is unrestricted after deployment: admin can rotate root mid-airdrop to redirect funds
Finds MerkleProof leaf format vulnerable to second-preimage (leaves not length-tagged / domain-separated)
Classifies missing claim-tracking or amount-in-leaf bug as Critical
Provides a Proof of Concept for at least one Critical/High finding
Provides a corrected code snippet (bitmap, keccak256(account, amount) leaf, or finalized-root pattern)
Anchors findings to checklist item IDs (SOL-XX-YY-N format)
Report includes Scope, Findings, and Recommendations sections

evals.prompt: Audit this smart contract for all security vulnerabilities. File: /Users/farrell/.claude-personal/skills/smart-contract-audit/evals/files/Bridge.sol
evals.expected_output: Complete audit report identifying: _lzReceive ignores _srcChainId (wrong-chain messages accepted), no per-source nonce / replay protection, mint() is public so anyone can mint tokens independent of bridge, trustedRemote comparison only covers single path (no per-chain mapping).
assertions: Finds _lzReceive does not validate _srcChainId (accepts messages from any source chain)
Finds mint() is public — anyone can mint tokens without going through _lzReceive
Finds no replay / nonce tracking on inbound messages
Finds trustedRemote is a single bytes blob rather than per-source-chain mapping (cannot safely support multiple sources)
Classifies public mint() OR missing srcChainId check as Critical
Provides a Proof of Concept (attack steps) for the public-mint or wrong-chain issue
Provides a corrected code snippet (make mint internal, add chainId+nonce mapping)
Anchors findings to checklist item IDs (SOL-XX-YY-N format)
Report includes Scope, Findings, and Recommendations sections

evals.prompt: Audit this smart contract for all security vulnerabilities. File: /Users/farrell/.claude-personal/skills/smart-contract-audit/evals/files/GovTimelock.sol
evals.expected_output: Complete audit report identifying: execute() bypasses delay when msg.sender == admin (admin executes arbitrary calls instantly), cancel() has no access control (anyone cancels any queued action), queue id uses abi.encodePacked (hash collisions across variable-length data), no guardian / veto role.
assertions: Finds execute() skips delay check when msg.sender == admin: defeats the purpose of the timelock
Finds cancel() has no access control: any address can cancel any queued action (griefing / blocking governance)
Finds queue id uses abi.encodePacked(target, value, data): hash collision possible because data is dynamic
Finds no guardian / veto role or missing separation-of-powers
Classifies admin-bypass OR open cancel() as Critical or High
Provides a Proof of Concept for at least one Critical/High finding
Provides a corrected code snippet (abi.encode, access-controlled cancel, remove admin fast-path)
Anchors findings to checklist item IDs (SOL-XX-YY-N format)
Report includes Scope, Findings, and Recommendations sections
